questions.question: 1. If a ship's metacentric height is increased, it will:

answers.a: Become more stable and roll faster.
answers.b: Become less stable and roll slower.
answers.c: Become more stable and roll slower.
answers.d: Become less stable and roll faster.

questions.question: 2. The metacentric height is affected by:

answers.a: Only the shape of the floating body.
answers.b: Only the density of the fluid.
answers.c: The shape of the floating body and the density of the fluid.
answers.d: The weight of the floating body.

questions.question: 3. For a rectangular pontoon, if the center of gravity is below the center of buoyancy, the metacentric height will be:
4. If the metacentric height is zero, the floating body is in:
5. In warships and racing yachts, the metacentric height is generally:

answers.a: Smaller than other categories of ships.
answers.b: Larger than other categories of ships.
answers.c: The same as other categories of ships.
